title: Ο περί Ηλεκτρονικών Επικοινωνιών και Ταχυδρομικών Υπηρεσιών Νόμος (Νόμος 112(Ι)/2004) — Ρυθμιστικές Διατάξεις Πληροφορικής
title_en: Electronic Communications and Postal Services Law (Law 112(I)/2004) — Information Technology Regulatory Provisions
description: This Law constitutes the primary regulatory framework for electronic communications in the Republic of Cyprus, transposing the European Union regulatory framework for electronic communications networks and services. It establishes the Office of the Commissioner of Electronic Communications and Postal Regulation as the national regulatory authority, sets out general authorisation conditions, and provides the regulatory basis for governing market access, consumer protection, and technical standards in the information technology and electronic communications sector.

provisions.title: Article 1. Object and Scope
provisions.content: This Law establishes a harmonised framework for the regulation of electronic communications networks and services, associated facilities and associated services, and certain aspects of terminal equipment. It transposes the European Union regulatory framework for electronic communications, comprising Directive 2002/21/EC (Framework Directive), Directive 2002/20/EC (Authorisation Directive), Directive 2002/19/EC (Access Directive), Directive 2002/22/EC (Universal Service Directive), and related directives as amended. The Law applies to all natural and legal persons providing electronic communications networks and services within the Republic of Cyprus, whether established in Cyprus or in another Member State, and establishes the regulatory authority responsible for its implementation.

provisions.title: Article 2. National Regulatory Authority
provisions.content: The Office of the Commissioner of Electronic Communications and Postal Regulation, established under this Law, constitutes the national regulatory authority for electronic communications in the Republic of Cyprus. The Commissioner is appointed by the Council of Ministers and shall exercise their functions independently and impartially, free from political or commercial influence. The Commissioner has the power to issue regulations, decisions, and guidelines within the scope of this Law; to investigate potential breaches of regulatory obligations; to impose remedies and administrative sanctions; and to cooperate with the Body of European Regulators for Electronic Communications (BEREC) and national regulatory authorities of other Member States.

provisions.title: Article 3. General Authorisation Framework
provisions.content: The provision of electronic communications networks or services shall be subject to a general authorisation which takes effect upon a notification submitted to the Commissioner. Providers shall not be required to obtain an explicit decision or administrative act from the Commissioner prior to commencing the provision of electronic communications networks or services, save where the use of radio frequencies or numbering resources requires a specific right. The general authorisation framework shall impose only conditions which are objectively justified in relation to the network or service concerned and shall be proportionate and non-discriminatory. Conditions applicable under a general authorisation shall be published and made publicly available in an accessible format.

provisions.title: Article 4. Rights of Use for Radio Frequencies
provisions.content: Where individual rights of use for radio frequencies are required, the Commissioner shall grant such rights upon application from any undertaking providing or intending to provide electronic communications networks or services, subject to the conditions set out in this Law. Rights of use shall be granted through open, transparent, non-discriminatory, and proportionate procedures and shall be subject to conditions that may include requirements relating to the quality, coverage, and rollout of networks, technical parameters to avoid interference, and obligations to facilitate spectrum sharing. The Commissioner shall manage the radio frequency spectrum in accordance with the applicable national frequency allocation plan and applicable Union law.

provisions.title: Article 5. Access and Interconnection
provisions.content: The Commissioner may impose on providers obligations relating to access and interconnection where this is necessary to ensure end-to-end connectivity, to promote competition in the provision of electronic communications services, or to protect the interests of end users. Obligations may include requirements to negotiate access in good faith, to maintain and make available specific types of access, to apply conditions related to access on an equivalent basis, or to implement cost orientation in access pricing. Providers with significant market power may be subject to additional obligations determined through market analysis procedures, including obligations regarding transparency, non-discrimination, accounting separation, and access to specific network elements and facilities.

provisions.title: Article 6. Universal Service Obligations
provisions.content: Universal service obligations shall ensure that all end users in the Republic of Cyprus have access to a defined minimum set of electronic communications services of specified quality at an affordable price, regardless of their geographic location. The Commissioner shall designate one or more undertakings to provide universal service elements and may impose specific obligations on designated undertakings in relation to the geographic coverage of service provision, the quality of service delivered, and the retail pricing of universal services. Designated undertakings shall be compensated for any net costs arising from universal service obligations that constitute an unfair burden, through mechanisms established and funded in accordance with applicable law.

provisions.title: Article 7. Consumer Protection
provisions.content: Providers of publicly available electronic communications services shall ensure that end users have access to adequate information about service quality, pricing, contract terms, and conditions. Contracts shall set out in a clear, comprehensive, and easily accessible form the minimum service quality levels offered, the nature and location of the services provided, and the means by which up-to-date information on applicable tariffs and maintenance charges may be obtained. End users shall have the right to withdraw from contracts without penalty upon notification of changes to contractual conditions proposed by the provider, and shall be entitled to have their complaints resolved in a timely manner through accessible dispute resolution mechanisms.

provisions.title: Article 8. Network and Information Security
provisions.content: Providers of public electronic communications networks and publicly available electronic communications services shall take appropriate technical and organisational measures to appropriately manage the security of their networks and services. These measures shall guarantee a level of security appropriate to the risk presented, having regard to the state of the art. In particular, measures to prevent and minimise the impact of security incidents on users and interconnected networks shall be adopted and maintained. The Commissioner, in coordination with the national cybersecurity authority, may specify minimum security requirements applicable to providers and may audit compliance with these requirements.

provisions.title: Article 9. Number Portability
provisions.content: Subscribers shall have the right to retain their number or numbers when changing providers of electronic communications services. The Commissioner shall establish and maintain provisions ensuring that number portability is available to all subscribers of publicly available telephone services, including mobile services, within the Republic of Cyprus. Porting of numbers shall be carried out as rapidly as possible, and in any case within one working day after the conclusion of the porting agreement. Providers involved in porting procedures shall cooperate technically and shall not use porting processes or procedures as means to deter subscribers from changing provider.

provisions.title: Article 10. Emergency Services
provisions.content: Providers of publicly available telephone services shall provide access to emergency services and shall transmit caller location information to emergency services, where technically feasible, in accordance with the specifications established by the Commissioner. The single European emergency number 112 shall be accessible free of charge from all terminal equipment, including mobile telephones, within the Republic of Cyprus. The Commissioner shall ensure that users with disabilities have access to emergency services on an equivalent basis to other users, and shall establish requirements for the availability of emergency services through alternative means of communication.

provisions.title: Article 11. Significant Market Power Analysis
provisions.content: The Commissioner shall carry out analyses of relevant markets in the electronic communications sector in accordance with the markets identified by the European Commission and using competition law principles. Where the Commissioner determines that a provider has significant market power in a relevant market, it may impose, maintain, amend, or withdraw obligations on that provider. Market analyses shall be conducted at regular intervals and shall be coordinated with the European Commission and other national regulatory authorities through established consultation procedures. Determinations of significant market power shall be proportionate to the nature of the market failure identified and the regulatory objective to be achieved.

provisions.title: Article 12. Accounting and Regulatory Reporting
provisions.content: The Commissioner may require providers, particularly those with significant market power, to implement accounting separation between different activities related to electronic communications services and to provide accounting information including cost accounting data. Providers subject to obligations of transparency shall publish a reference offer containing a description of the relevant offerings broken down into components according to market needs and the associated terms and conditions including prices. The Commissioner shall specify the format and content of regulatory accounting information to be submitted and may engage independent auditors to verify the accuracy of reported data.

provisions.title: Article 13. Dispute Resolution
provisions.content: In the event of a dispute arising between providers of electronic communications networks or services in connection with obligations arising under this Law, any party to the dispute may refer the matter to the Commissioner for resolution. The Commissioner shall encourage the resolution of disputes through negotiation and, where parties cannot reach agreement, shall issue a binding decision within four months of the referral being made to it. Decisions of the Commissioner shall be published subject to requirements of commercial confidentiality. Any person aggrieved by a decision of the Commissioner may appeal to the competent court within thirty days of notification of the decision.

provisions.title: Article 14. Spectrum Management
provisions.content: The Commissioner, in coordination with the Ministry responsible for communications, shall manage the use of radio frequencies allocated to electronic communications services in accordance with the national frequency allocation plan and applicable Union law. Spectrum management policies shall aim to promote competition, efficient use of spectrum, and the development of innovative services. The Commissioner shall consider technical harmonisation measures adopted by the European Commission and may participate in multilateral or bilateral coordination arrangements with spectrum management authorities of other states to address issues of interference or cross-border spectrum use.

provisions.title: Article 15. Administrative Sanctions
provisions.content: The Commissioner may impose administrative sanctions on providers who fail to comply with the provisions of this Law or with obligations, conditions, decisions, or guidelines issued thereunder. Administrative sanctions shall be proportionate to the nature, gravity, and duration of the breach, and may include fines, suspension of rights, or revocation of specific authorisations or rights of use. Before imposing an administrative sanction, the Commissioner shall give the provider concerned the opportunity to make representations regarding the alleged breach. The Commissioner shall publish decisions imposing administrative sanctions subject to requirements of commercial confidentiality and protection of personal data.

provisions.title: Article 16. Infrastructure Sharing
provisions.content: The Commissioner may encourage and, where appropriate, impose the sharing of infrastructure, including passive physical infrastructure such as ducts, masts, buildings, and street cabinets, where this would benefit efficient use of physical infrastructure and avoid unnecessary duplication. Conditions for infrastructure sharing shall be determined by the Commissioner following consultation with market participants and shall aim to be fair, reasonable, and non-discriminatory. The Commissioner may require providers in possession of information on the location and capacity of existing infrastructure to make such information available to other providers seeking to conduct an assessment of the feasibility of infrastructure sharing.

definitions.definition: Transmission systems and, where applicable, switching or routing equipment and other resources, including network elements which are not active, which permit the conveyance of signals by wire, radio, optical or other electromagnetic means.

The Office of the Commissioner of Electronic Communications and Postal Regulation, designated as the body responsible for applying the regulatory framework for electronic communications in the Republic of Cyprus.
A position equivalent to dominance, as defined in accordance with competition law principles, held by a provider in a relevant product and geographic market in the electronic communications sector.

The minimum set of services of specified quality which is available to all end users regardless of their geographical location and, in the light of specific national conditions, at an affordable price.
